title: Bat Removal & Exclusion in West Brighton, Staten Island | $900-$2,500
metaDescription: Professional bat removal and exclusion services in West Brighton, Staten Island. Humane bat control for homes and businesses. Licensed experts. Call for free inspection!
intro: West Brighton residents facing bat infestations need safe, humane, and effective solutions that comply with New York wildlife regulations. Our bat removal and exclusion services protect your West Brighton home or business from these protected but problematic animals. We specialize in complete bat removal, guano cleanup, and permanent exclusion to keep your property bat-free.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC brings over a decade of experience serving West Brighton and Staten Island with specialized bat removal services that prioritize both homeowner safety and humane wildlife practices. Our licensed technicians understand the unique architecture of West Brighton homes and provide comprehensive solutions with warranties to ensure bats don't return.
localContext: West Brighton's mix of older Victorian homes, mid-century residential properties near Clove Lakes Park, and buildings along Victory Boulevard often provide ideal entry points for bats seeking roosts in attics, soffits, and chimneys. The tree-lined streets and proximity to Clove Lakes Park create perfect bat habitats, making professional exclusion essential for area homeowners.

faqs.question: How much does bat removal cost in West Brighton?
faqs.answer: Bat removal and exclusion services in West Brighton typically range from $900 to $2,500, depending on the size of the colony, number of entry points, extent of damage, and accessibility of the roost. We provide free inspections and detailed estimates before beginning any work.

faqs.question: Is bat removal legal in Staten Island?
faqs.answer: Yes, but bats are protected in New York, so removal must be done humanely using exclusion methods rather than extermination. We follow all New York State DEC regulations, which prohibit bat exclusion during maternity season (roughly May through August) when flightless young bats may be present.

faqs.question: How do I know if I have bats in my West Brighton home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include squeaking or scratching sounds at dusk or dawn, bat droppings (guano) in attics or near entry points, brown staining around holes or gaps in your roofline, and seeing bats enter or exit your home at sunset. A strong ammonia-like odor from accumulated guano is another telltale sign.

faqs.question: What does the bat exclusion process involve?
faqs.answer: Our comprehensive service includes a thorough inspection to locate all entry points, installation of one-way exclusion devices that allow bats to leave but not return, sealing all secondary entry points, guano cleanup and sanitization, insulation replacement if needed, and a warranty period to ensure the exclusion is successful.
